headline: International Paper announces shutdowns, layoffs at 4 US facilities
summary: After a relatively quiet second quarter so far, the company has resumed its 20-month string of facility closure and layoff announcements affecting more than 5,800 employees.

raw.headline: International Paper announces shutdowns, layoffs at 4 US facilities
raw.summary: After a relatively quiet second quarter so far, the company has resumed its 20-month string of facility closure and layoff announcements affecting more than 5,800 employees.

headline: International Paper to Shut Four North American Facilities by Third Quarter of 2026 (IP)
summary: International Paper (NYSE:IP) has announced plans to close four facilities across North America by the end of the third quarter of 2026 as part of efforts to optimise its manufacturing network and improve long-term competitiveness. According to a press release issued on 26 June 2026, the company will discontinue preprint operations at its Richwood, Kentucky facility, close its sheet plant in Aurora, Illinois, and cease operations at its converting plants in Elk Grove, California, and Barrington,

headline: International Paper to shut more US facilities
summary: The closures are intended to streamline network and direct spending towards “highest-value” areas, the company said.
